adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain a geopolitical risk register that is reviewed
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Risk Officer must formally re-rate Mali in the enterprise geopolitical risk register to Severity 4 (Critical) by close of business May 21, 2026, citing the April 25 JNIM-FLA offensive, the May 19–20 Bamako road blockade, and the confirmed killing of Defence Minister Gen. Sadio Camara as material escalation triggers. The updated register entry must document the withdrawal of Russian forces from northern positions as a compounding factor reducing junta stabilisation capacity, and must flag dependency exposure across all supply routes transiting Senegal and Ivory Coast into Bamako.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a review on material escalation; this adaptation specifies the three discrete trigger events from the May 2026 window — the JNIM-FLA offensive, the ministerial assassination, and Russian force withdrawal — that together constitute the threshold, preventing the register update from being deferred pending a single 'confirmed collapse' signal.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and maintain personnel safety and evacuation protocols
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Head of Security (or delegated regional security manager) must conduct a headcount and welfare confirmation of all personnel located in or within 72-hour travel distance of Bamako by 23:59 UTC May 20, 2026, and must issue a Stage 2 Restricted Movement directive prohibiting non-essential road transit on the Bamako–Dakar (RN3) and Bamako–Abidjan (RN7) corridors, which JNIM has confirmed are under checkpoint control as of May 19. Any evacuation of non-essential staff must route exclusively via Bamako-Sénou International Airport (BKO) while the airport remains operationally accessible, with commercial airline availability assessed every 12 hours.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control establishes the existence of evacuation protocols; this adaptation names the two specific blocked road corridors under JNIM checkpoint control, designates the airport as the sole viable egress point, and sets a 12-hour reassessment cadence matched to the rapid operational tempo of the blockade.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Define and exercise supply-chain continuity procedures for critical
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Head of Supply Chain must, by May 23, 2026, quantify on-hand inventory of all critical inputs that ordinarily transit the Bamako–Dakar or Bamako–Abidjan corridors, express this as days-of-cover at current consumption rates, and present a written continuity plan to the COO identifying alternative sourcing or air-freight options for any input with fewer than 30 days of cover remaining. The plan must assume the road blockade persists for a minimum of 60 days — consistent with ACLED's May 2026 assessment that the junta's survival is contingent on Russian military presence, which has itself retreated — and must not assume restoration of ground-route access as a planning variable before that horizon.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires continuity procedures for conflict-affected regions; this adaptation anchors the planning assumption to ACLED's specific regime-fragility assessment and the demonstrated Russian withdrawal, preventing an optimistic short-duration assumption that would render the continuity plan operationally useless.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain engagement protocols with host-government counterparts and relevant
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Government Affairs lead responsible for Mali must, within five business days of May 20, 2026, assess whether the Goïta junta retains sufficient administrative control of Bamako to honour existing operating licences, permits, or concessions, and must simultaneously open or reconfirm a direct communication channel with ECOWAS and any UN MINUSMA successor mission that retains a Mali mandate, to preserve a non-junta engagement track. All contact logs with Malian ministry counterparts must be time-stamped and archived, and any communications gap exceeding 72 hours with a previously active government counterpart must be escalated to the Chief Risk Officer as a potential licence-access deterioration signal.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control addresses host-government engagement; this adaptation recognises the specific dual-authority risk — that the Goïta junta may lose effective territorial control of Bamako to JNIM, making continued exclusive engagement with junta ministries a single point of failure — and mandates parallel intergovernmental tracks to preserve access continuity.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Ensure Board-level crisis reporting mechanisms are activated when
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Executive Officer must convene an unscheduled Board-level crisis briefing no later than May 22, 2026, presenting a structured impact assessment that covers: (i) personnel exposure in the Bamako metropolitan area and on the Senegal and Ivory Coast supply corridors; (ii) enterprise value implications of a sustained 60-day Bamako blockade on Mali-dependent revenues or operations; and (iii) reputational and regulatory exposure arising from continued commercial association with the Goïta junta under a Severity 4 designation. The Board must record a formal resolution either approving continuation of Mali operations under the continuity plan or directing controlled wind-down, with that resolution minuted and retained for regulatory purposes.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires Board-level crisis reporting at a defined severity threshold; this adaptation specifies the three board-agenda dimensions uniquely relevant to a junta-collapse scenario — personnel, economic continuity, and reputational association with a potentially collapsing regime — and mandates a recorded resolution to prevent governance paralysis during the escalation window.

incidents.headline: China retaliates against EU 20th Russia sanctions; lists European defence firms
incidents.summary: On April 23, 2026, the EU adopted its 20th Russia sanctions package, extending measures to third-country entities including firms in China and Hong Kong. Within days, China responded by placing several European defence companies on its export control list and warning of further countermeasures. This represents a direct escalation in the EU–China sanctions cycle, with Chinese listings carrying extra-territorial reach prohibiting foreign entities from transferring China-origin items to the listed companies, materially raising compliance costs for European and global defence supply chains.
incidents.entity: European Union (and targeted European defence companies)

secondary_mappings.why: China's retaliatory export control listings on European defence firms have explicit extra-territorial application, disrupting dual-use and defence supply chains globally.
Both the EU 20th sanctions package and China's responsive export-control designations constitute binding regulatory enforcement actions with immediate compliance obligations.
Extra-territorial reach of China's listings creates concentrated supply chain risk for any firm globally that sources China-origin components destined for listed European entities.

incidents.severity_rationale: Reciprocal sanctions-and-export-controls escalation between two of the world's largest trading blocs affects multiple listed European defence companies and raises systemic dual-use compliance risk; severity moderated because no physical conflict is directly involved.

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure the organisation can identify, assess, and respond to geopolitical sanctions and export-control actions that materially restrict its access to critical inputs, markets, or operational partners.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Maintain a current geopolitical risk register covering sanctions exposure, export-control designations, and cross-border regulatory restrictions relevant to the organisation's supply chain and operating jurisdictions.
Establish and test contingency sourcing and logistics plans for critical inputs subject to geopolitical supply disruption.
Implement a monitoring programme for foreign government retaliatory measures, including extra-territorial export controls, that may affect the organisation's supply chain or trading partners.
Define escalation and crisis response procedures for material geopolitical events affecting operational continuity or regulatory compliance status.
Engage external geopolitical and trade-law counsel to assess and respond to novel or escalatory government-imposed restrictions on the organisation's sector.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain a current geopolitical risk register covering sanctions exposure
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Within five business days of China's April 2026 export-control listings, the Head of Trade Compliance must update the organisation's geopolitical risk register to record each named European defence company as a designated entity under China's export-control framework, flag any China-origin components (including dual-use electronics, rare-earth inputs, and precision tooling) currently transiting those companies' supply chains, and assign a red-rated exposure score reflecting the extra-territorial prohibition on third-country transfers. Evidence of completion is a dated register entry reviewed and signed off by the Chief Risk Officer, with a distribution record to procurement, legal, and the relevant business-unit heads.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires a current register of sanctions exposure; this adaptation forces immediate entry of China's specific retaliatory listings against named EU defence companies — a novel extra-territorial export-control vector not captured by the EU's own Russia sanctions register — and mandates the extra-territorial transfer prohibition as a discrete exposure category.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement a monitoring programme for foreign government retaliatory measures
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Trade Compliance function must configure its regulatory monitoring programme to track China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) and General Administration of Customs announcement channels on a daily basis for additions to the entity list published in response to the EU's 20th Russia sanctions package, with an automated alert threshold set to trigger within 24 hours of any new listing affecting a current supplier, customer, or logistics intermediary. Each triggered alert must be routed simultaneously to the Head of Trade Compliance, General Counsel, and the relevant category procurement lead, with a mandatory 48-hour response window to assess operational exposure. Evidence of completion is a monitoring configuration log, a documented alert-routing matrix, and a test alert run no later than 30 April 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control establishes a monitoring programme for retaliatory extra-territorial controls; this adaptation specifically targets MOFCOM's incremental listing mechanism activated against EU defence firms in April 2026 and imposes a 24-hour detection and 48-hour response cadence calibrated to the pace at which China has signalled further countermeasures will follow.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and test contingency sourcing and logistics plans
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Within ten business days, the Chief Procurement Officer must commission a bill-of-materials audit across all product lines with China-origin content exceeding 10% by value, identifying every component sourced through or from suppliers that are either currently listed by MOFCOM or that transact with listed European defence companies, and must produce a ranked substitution shortlist — with lead times and cost deltas — for any item assessed as critical (i.e. no qualified alternative supplier available within 90 days). The resulting contingency sourcing plan must be stress-tested against a scenario in which China expands its April 2026 listings to cover at least three additional EU-headquartered defence primes, and the plan must be approved by the Executive Committee no later than 30 business days from the date of China's initial retaliatory listings. Evidence is the completed audit report, the ranked substitution shortlist, the stress-test scenario record, and the signed Executive Committee approval minute.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires contingency sourcing plans for geopolitically disrupted inputs; this adaptation forces a forward-looking stress test against China's stated intent to escalate beyond its initial April 2026 listings, rather than treating the current list as the ceiling of exposure.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Define escalation and crisis response procedures for material
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Risk Officer must convene an emergency response review within three business days of China's MOFCOM listings to determine whether the organisation's regulatory compliance status is materially affected — specifically assessing whether any current contractual obligation requires delivery of China-origin goods to, or receipt of China-origin goods from, a MOFCOM-listed European defence company, which would constitute a prohibited transfer. If a prohibited transfer is identified, the General Counsel must issue a stop-shipment directive within 24 hours and notify the relevant national export-control authority in the organisation's home jurisdiction within the timeframe required by that authority's regulations. A crisis-response log recording every decision, directive, and notification, with timestamps and accountable signatories, is the mandatory evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control defines escalation procedures for geopolitical events affecting compliance status; this adaptation addresses the specific extra-territorial prohibited-transfer failure mode created by China's April 2026 retaliatory listings, where a European company could unknowingly become a sanctions violator by executing a pre-existing contract rather than through any new commercial decision.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Engage external geopolitical and trade-law counsel to assess
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Within five business days of China's April 2026 retaliatory listings, the General Counsel must retain external counsel with dual expertise in Chinese export-control law (specifically MOFCOM's Unreliable Entity List and Export Control Law mechanisms) and EU trade law to provide a written legal opinion covering: (a) the precise scope of the extra-territorial transfer prohibition as it applies to the organisation's current and pipeline transactions; (b) any licensing or exemption pathways available under China's Export Control Law; and (c) the interaction between the EU's 20th Russia sanctions package obligations and China's countermeasures where they create conflicting compliance duties. The opinion must be delivered no later than fifteen business days from the date of instruction, reviewed at a quorum of the organisation's Risk and Compliance Committee, and translated into binding internal compliance guidance within five business days of receipt. Evidence is the instruction letter with scope, the dated legal opinion, the Committee review minute, and the issued compliance guidance.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control calls for external trade-law counsel on novel restrictions; this adaptation is specific to the legal novelty of China's extra-territorial prohibited-transfer mechanism as deployed against EU defence firms in April 2026, which creates a dual-jurisdiction compliance conflict not resolvable by standard sanctions counsel unfamiliar with MOFCOM's enforcement architecture.

incidents.headline: TeamPCP compromises Microsoft durabletask PyPI SDK on May 19
incidents.summary: On 2026-05-19, threat actor TeamPCP published three backdoored versions (v1.4.1, v1.4.2, v1.4.3) of Microsoft's official durabletask Python SDK to PyPI within a 35-minute window, using a stolen PyPI API token obtained from an earlier supply chain attack; the package serves as the official SDK for Azure Durable Functions and receives over 400,000 downloads per month. The malicious payload silently downloads a second-stage credential harvester ('rope.pyz') targeting AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, password managers, and over 90 developer tool configurations, then spreads laterally through cloud infrastructure. PyPI quarantined all three versions following analysis published by Wiz Research and StepSecurity on 2026-05-19; GitHub was not breached — the attacker bypassed CI/CD entirely by uploading directly via a stolen long-lived token.

secondary_mappings.why: Attacker injected malicious code into an official published software package distributed via a public package registry, exploiting stolen API tokens to bypass CI/CD controls.
Payload exfiltrated cloud credentials (AWS, Azure, GCP) and developer secrets to attacker-controlled C2 infrastructure outside the victim's jurisdiction.
Customers of Azure Durable Functions using the compromised SDK faced immediate operational disruption requiring emergency credential rotation and re-pinning of the package.

incidents.severity_rationale: Official SDK for a major cloud platform poisoned via a stolen credential; 400,000 monthly downloads exposed to a multi-cloud credential stealer with lateral movement and persistence; part of the most prolific supply chain campaign of 2026.

sources.title: durabletask: TeamPCP's Latest PyPi Compromise
Microsoft's durabletask PyPI Package Compromised in Supply Chain Attack
Malicious durabletask on PyPI: Multi-Cloud Credential Stealer with Worm Capabilities

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure third-party and open-source package publishing credentials are scoped, time-limited, and protected against theft and misuse through stolen long-lived tokens.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Establish and enforce a third-party software and package integrity verification programme covering all open-source dependencies published under organisational namespaces.
Implement credential lifecycle management controls for all CI/CD and package registry publishing tokens, including time-to-live limits and automated rotation.
Enforce least-privilege access for all supply chain tooling, preventing CI runner credentials from accumulating cross-service publishing rights.
Deploy real-time monitoring and anomaly detection across package registries to identify unauthorised or out-of-band publication events.
Maintain an authoritative software bill of materials (SBOM) for all published packages and validate integrity of releases against approved build provenance records.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement credential lifecycle management controls for all CI/CD
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Microsoft's PyPI publishing token for the durabletask namespace must be revoked and reissued immediately, with the replacement token scoped exclusively to the durabletask package, granted a maximum time-to-live of 30 days, and stored in a secrets manager (e.g. Azure Key Vault) rather than as a long-lived personal credential. The Python SDK team lead (or designated release engineer) is accountable for completing revocation and reissuance within 24 hours of this advisory; evidence of completion is a PyPI token audit log showing the compromised token deactivated and a Key Vault access policy showing the replacement token stored and rotation scheduled.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The incident was enabled by a single long-lived PyPI API token obtained via a prior supply chain attack; this control names the specific token scope, storage mechanism, and 30-day TTL required to prevent the exact credential-persistence failure mode observed on 2026-05-19.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Deploy real-time monitoring and anomaly detection across package
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Microsoft must integrate an automated publication-event monitor on the durabletask PyPI namespace (via PyPI's event webhooks or a polling agent such as StepSecurity's package monitor) that pages the Azure SDK release team within 15 minutes whenever a new version is published outside of an approved GitHub Actions release workflow run ID. The monitor must be operational within 72 hours of this advisory; evidence of completion is a test alert triggered by a synthetic out-of-band publication event and a documented incident-response runbook entry for durabletask PyPI anomalies.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: TeamPCP published three malicious versions within a 35-minute window entirely outside GitHub CI/CD, meaning no existing pipeline gate fired; this control targets the specific bypass vector — direct PyPI upload — and the specific 35-minute detection gap observed on 2026-05-19.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain an authoritative software bill of materials (SBOM)
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Microsoft must publish a Sigstore-signed SBOM and SLSA provenance attestation for every durabletask release beginning with the next clean version published after quarantine of v1.4.1–v1.4.3, and must configure PyPI's Trusted Publisher mechanism so that only a designated GitHub Actions workflow in the microsoft/durabletask-python repository is authorised to publish to the durabletask namespace. The Azure SDK governance lead is accountable; evidence of completion is a verifiable Sigstore bundle for the next release and a PyPI Trusted Publisher configuration record showing no manual token upload path remaining.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The attacker bypassed build provenance entirely by using a stolen token to upload directly to PyPI, meaning consumers had no attestation to verify against; this control closes that specific gap by binding publication rights to a named workflow and attaching verifiable provenance to every subsequent durabletask artifact.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and enforce a third-party software and package
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: All Azure Durable Functions deployments and CI/CD pipelines consuming the durabletask Python SDK must pin to a verified clean version (v1.4.0 or the next attested release post-quarantine) within 48 hours of this advisory, and must validate the package hash at install time against the known-good SHA-256 digest published in Microsoft's security advisory; any environment where v1.4.1, v1.4.2, or v1.4.3 was installed must be treated as fully compromised, with credential rotation for all AWS, Azure, GCP, and Kubernetes service accounts and password-manager secrets accessible from that environment completed within the same 48-hour window. The accountable owner is the Azure Durable Functions service engineering lead, with evidence provided as a dependency scan report showing zero instances of v1.4.1–v1.4.3 across all environments and a credential rotation log timestamped within the 48-hour deadline.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The backdoored versions (v1.4.1–v1.4.3) silently deployed a credential harvester ('rope.pyz') targeting the specific cloud and developer-tool surfaces named in the incident; this control operationalises the remediation scope around those exact three versions and their known harvesting targets rather than addressing package integrity generically.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Enforce least-privilege access for all supply chain tooling
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Microsoft must audit all GitHub Actions workflows and service principals in the microsoft/durabletask-python repository within 5 business days to confirm that no single credential or runner identity holds simultaneous write access to both the PyPI durabletask namespace and any Azure, AWS, or GCP production environment; any identity found to hold cross-service publishing rights must be split into separate scoped identities before the audit closes. The Azure SDK security architect is accountable; evidence of completion is an access-rights matrix reviewed and signed off by that role, showing no remaining combined PyPI-publisher-plus-cloud-producer identity.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The incident demonstrates that a single stolen token was sufficient to publish to PyPI and that the payload then spread laterally through cloud infrastructure, indicating that cross-service credential accumulation amplified the blast radius; this control directly targets the privilege boundary failure between PyPI publishing rights and cloud environment access in the specific microsoft/durabletask-python context.

incidents.headline: TeamPCP lists GitHub source code for sale; GitHub investigates breach
incidents.summary: On 2026-05-20, GitHub (a Microsoft subsidiary) disclosed it is investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the threat actor TeamPCP listed GitHub's source code and internal organisations for sale on a cybercrime forum for no less than $50,000. GitHub stated it had 'no evidence of impact to customer information stored outside of GitHub's internal repositories' but confirmed it is monitoring for follow-on activity and will notify customers through established incident-response channels if any downstream impact is discovered. The disclosure coincides with the ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign in which stolen credentials from earlier TeamPCP open-source package compromises (TanStack, durabletask, @antv) are assessed to have provided the initial access vector.

secondary_mappings.why: GitHub's own internal repositories were accessed through credentials stolen via upstream supply chain compromises, representing a cascading fourth-party breach of a foundational developer infrastructure provider.
Public listing of GitHub source code for sale on a cybercrime forum constitutes a high-visibility reputational event for the platform underpinning global software development.
GitHub serves as critical shared infrastructure for millions of open-source and enterprise software pipelines; any confirmed breach carries systemic downstream risk.

incidents.severity_rationale: GitHub is foundational shared infrastructure for global software development; a claimed internal repository breach by an active extortion group carrying valid stolen credentials is a high-severity systemic risk event, even though confirmed customer-data impact has not yet been established.

sources.title: GitHub Investigating TeamPCP Claimed Breach of ~4,000 Internal Repositories
TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17

adaptive_objectives.statement: Detect, contain, and eradicate unauthorized third-party or threat-actor access to proprietary source code repositories and CI/CD pipeline infrastructure before material exfiltration or downstream supply-chain weaponisation occurs.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Establish and maintain continuous monitoring of third-party and insider access to source code repositories, including anomaly detection on clone, archive, and mass-download operations.
Implement privileged access management controls for all CI/CD pipeline service accounts, including scoped token issuance, mandatory rotation schedules, and just-in-time access provisioning.
Maintain a software supply chain bill of materials (SBOM) and enforce integrity verification for all third-party extensions, plugins, and packages integrated into the development toolchain.
Define and exercise an incident response playbook specific to source-code exfiltration events, including pre-authorised secret-rotation runbooks and stakeholder notification chains.
Conduct periodic threat-intelligence-led red-team exercises simulating supply-chain credential-harvesting attacks against CI/CD infrastructure and developer endpoint tooling.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and maintain continuous monitoring of third-party and
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: GitHub's Security Incident Response Team (SIRT) must, within 24 hours of this disclosure (by 2026-05-21 23:59 UTC), produce a forensic access log review covering all clone, archive, and bulk-download operations against GitHub's internal repositories for the 90-day window preceding 2026-05-20, filtered specifically for service-account and OAuth-token activity associated with the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign's known credential artifacts (those derived from the TanStack, durabletask, and @antv package compromises). The review must identify every repository touched, the volume of data transferred, and the account identity behind each operation, with findings delivered to GitHub's VP of Security and Microsoft's CISO in a written report. Evidence of completion is the timestamped forensic report, a confirmed log-ingestion receipt from GitHub's SIEM, and sign-off from both named executives.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires continuous anomaly detection broadly; this adaptation targets the specific 90-day pre-disclosure window, the specific credential lineage from the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign, and the specific internal-repository perimeter GitHub confirmed was accessed, producing a bounded and evidenced forensic artifact rather than an ongoing monitoring posture.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement privileged access management controls for all CI/CD
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: GitHub's Platform Engineering lead must, within 6 hours of this disclosure (by 2026-05-21 05:59 UTC), initiate emergency rotation of all CI/CD pipeline service-account tokens and OAuth application credentials scoped to GitHub's internal repositories, prioritising tokens issued to or consumable by the package ecosystems involved in the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign (npm registry integrations, GitHub Actions workflows referencing TanStack, durabletask, or @antv dependencies, and any service account whose credential hash appears in TeamPCP's known indicators of compromise). Rotated tokens must be re-issued under a scoped, just-in-time model with a maximum 8-hour TTL until a full credential audit is completed. Evidence of completion is a signed rotation manifest listing each revoked token ID, its original scope, its replacement TTL, and the approving engineer's identity, submitted to GitHub SIRT.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control specifies rotation schedules and JIT provisioning as standing controls; this adaptation mandates emergency out-of-cycle rotation within a specific deadline, scoped to the exact credential classes assessed as the initial-access vector in this incident, with a temporary TTL constraint that the generic control does not prescribe.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain a software supply chain bill of materials
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: GitHub's Supply Chain Security team must, within 48 hours of this disclosure (by 2026-05-22 23:59 UTC), generate a differential SBOM comparison for all GitHub-internal toolchain dependencies that reference or transitively depend on the three compromised packages (TanStack, durabletask, @antv) as of 2026-05-20, and validate the integrity hashes of currently deployed versions against the pre-compromise baseline recorded in GitHub's dependency graph. Any package version that cannot be hash-verified against a known-good baseline must be quarantined from GitHub's internal build pipelines immediately, with re-admission requiring a manual code review and a new signed provenance attestation. Evidence of completion is the differential SBOM report, the list of quarantined packages with quarantine timestamps, and attestations for any packages cleared for re-admission.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires maintaining an SBOM and enforcing integrity verification generally; this adaptation operationalises that requirement as a targeted differential check against the three specifically named compromised packages at the precise date of this incident, adding a quarantine-and-re-admission gate that the generic control does not define.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Define and exercise an incident response playbook specific
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: GitHub's SIRT Incident Commander must, within 12 hours of this disclosure (by 2026-05-21 11:59 UTC), formally activate the source-code exfiltration playbook and complete the pre-authorised secret-rotation runbook for all secrets stored in GitHub's internal repositories, including but not limited to signing keys, deployment credentials, and internal API tokens, documenting each rotated secret by type, owning team, and rotation timestamp. In parallel, the Incident Commander must trigger the stakeholder notification chain for GitHub Enterprise and GitHub Actions customers whose workflows may reference internal-repository artifacts, using GitHub's established incident-response communication channels (status.github.com and direct customer security advisories), with an initial customer notification issued no later than 2026-05-22 18:00 UTC regardless of whether downstream impact has been confirmed. Evidence of completion is the activated playbook record with timestamped runbook entries, the rotation log, and the published or draft customer advisory with send timestamp.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control defines the requirement for a playbook and pre-authorised runbooks in the abstract; this adaptation specifies the activation deadline, the exact secret categories requiring rotation given GitHub's internal-repository scope, and the customer notification deadline tied to GitHub's own public disclosure statement that notification will occur through established channels if downstream impact is discovered.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Conduct periodic threat-intelligence-led red-team exercises simulating supply-chain credential-harvesting
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: GitHub's Red Team (or a pre-contracted adversary simulation vendor) must, within 30 days of this disclosure (by 2026-06-19), execute a targeted threat-intelligence-led simulation replicating the Mini Shai-Hulud initial-access vector: specifically, a scenario in which a malicious version of a widely used npm package (modelled on the TanStack, durabletask, or @antv compromise pattern) harvests developer-endpoint credentials and uses them to authenticate to GitHub's internal CI/CD pipeline and internal repository infrastructure. The exercise must attempt lateral movement from a compromised developer token to bulk repository cloning, and must test whether GitHub's anomaly detection and SIRT escalation procedures would have detected and contained the activity within the dwell time observed in this incident. A written after-action report identifying detection gaps and control failures must be delivered to GitHub's VP of Security and the Microsoft Security Response Center within 7 days of exercise completion. Evidence of completion is the signed exercise plan, red-team activity logs, and the final after-action report with attributed detection gaps.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires periodic red-team exercises simulating supply-chain credential-harvesting attacks in general; this adaptation prescribes a specific scenario constructed from the exact attack chain observed in this incident (Mini Shai-Hulud, named compromised packages, developer-endpoint credential harvesting targeting GitHub-internal CI/CD), with a 30-day execution deadline that transforms the periodic cadence into an immediate post-incident validation.

incidents.headline: TeamPCP Mini Shai-Hulud worm re-emerges May 19: @antv, GitHub Actions, VSCode hit
incidents.summary: On 2026-05-19, Wiz Research confirmed a new wave of the TeamPCP Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain worm targeting Alibaba's AntV data-visualisation npm ecosystem (size-sensor, echarts-for-react, timeago.js and hundreds of @antv-scoped packages), the GitHub Action actions-cool/issues-helper, and a VSCode extension, with stolen CI/CD secrets being dumped to thousands of public GitHub repositories. A compromised maintainer account — identified as responsible for prior @antv wave repositories — was observed targeting further open-source projects, with three new malicious package waves confirmed within the 24-hour window. The coordinated multi-ecosystem compromise expands the blast radius of the ongoing 2026 Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, which by this wave had accumulated over 637 malicious versions across 317 packages with combined downstream reach exceeding 15 million weekly downloads.

secondary_mappings.why: Attacker used a compromised npm maintainer account to publish 637 malicious versions across 317 packages, injecting credential-stealing payloads into widely-used open-source libraries.

The @antv ecosystem serves as a single-source data-visualisation dependency for many enterprise applications; compromise of the entire namespace creates concentrated downstream exposure for consuming organisations.

incidents.severity_rationale: Mass poisoning of a widely-used data-visualisation npm namespace with credential-stealing worm payloads affecting 15M+ weekly downloads, but remediation was rapid and no confirmed large-scale production exploitation was disclosed within the window.

adaptive_objectives.statement: Detect and contain third-party software supply chain compromises that introduce malicious code into development pipelines through trusted open-source package maintainer accounts.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Maintain an approved third-party software component inventory with integrity verification at each ingestion point.
Enforce security review and approval gates for all third-party software updates prior to pipeline integration.
Implement continuous monitoring of third-party components for indicators of compromise or unexpected behavioural change.
Establish and maintain a third-party incident response procedure with defined escalation paths and notification obligations.
Require credential rotation and access revocation procedures for all third-party integration points upon detection of supply chain compromise.

incidents.if_you_operate_x_then_y: If you operate a CI/CD pipeline that installs any @antv, echarts-for-react, timeago.js, or size-sensor npm packages, you should immediately pin all dependencies to pre-May-19 SHA-locked versions, revoke and rotate every secret accessible to those runners, and audit GitHub Actions logs for connections to filev2.getsession.org or api.masscan.cloud within the next 24-72 hours.

velocity_signal.pattern: Self-propagating npm supply-chain worm — multi-ecosystem credential harvesting

incidents.emerging_risk_signal: TeamPCP's open-sourcing of the Shai-Hulud worm on BreachForums means copycat actors can now run independent waves, making attribution-dependent defences structurally obsolete within weeks.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain an approved third-party software component inventory with
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Within 24 hours of this advisory, the package management team must generate a full dependency inventory of all @antv-scoped packages, size-sensor, echarts-for-react, and timeago.js pinned in any internal pipeline, CI/CD environment, or VSCode extension bundle, cross-referencing each version hash against the 317 known-malicious package list published by Wiz Research on 2026-05-19. Any package version not matching a pre-19 May integrity baseline must be flagged as compromised and quarantined pending replacement. The accountable owner is the Head of Platform Engineering; evidence of completion is a signed inventory report with SHA-256 digests and a clear/flagged status per entry, submitted to the Security Operations Centre by end of business the same day.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires a general approved-component inventory; this adaptation specifically targets the 317 malicious @antv and associated package versions confirmed in the Mini Shai-Hulud wave and sets a 24-hour quarantine deadline anchored to the 2026-05-19 compromise window, which the generic control does not prescribe.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Enforce security review and approval gates for all
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Effective immediately and until further notice, all GitHub Actions pipeline steps that reference actions-cool/issues-helper must be pinned to a commit SHA predating 2026-05-19 00:00 UTC and must pass a mandatory security review by the Application Security team before any update is permitted; use of a floating version tag (e.g. @v1, @main) is prohibited. Additionally, any pull request introducing or updating an @antv-scoped npm package or the VSCode extension inventory must be blocked by an automated policy gate until the package version is confirmed absent from the Wiz Research 2026-05-19 malicious-version list. The pipeline owner and the Application Security Lead are jointly accountable; evidence of completion is a policy-gate configuration commit merged to the main branch with test results showing the block firing on a synthetic malicious version, retained in the change management system.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires security gates for third-party updates generically; this adaptation names the specific GitHub Action (actions-cool/issues-helper), the @antv npm scope, and the VSCode extension vector identified in this wave, and introduces a hard floating-tag prohibition tied to the 2026-05-19 compromise date.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement continuous monitoring of third-party components for indicators
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Security Operations team must deploy or update runtime behavioural monitoring rules — within 48 hours of this advisory — to alert on any process spawned by an @antv-scoped package, size-sensor, echarts-for-react, or timeago.js that attempts outbound network connections, writes to CI/CD secret stores, or accesses GitHub Actions runner environment variables; these indicators match the exfiltration behaviour confirmed by Wiz Research whereby stolen secrets were dumped to public GitHub repositories. Monitoring coverage must extend to VSCode extension host processes within developer workstations enrolled in the corporate endpoint management platform. The CISO is accountable; evidence of completion is a rule deployment report with at least one synthetic test trigger per indicator logged in the SIEM, completed within the 48-hour window.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires continuous monitoring for indicators of compromise broadly; this adaptation specifies the exact behavioural signatures — secret-store access and public-repo exfiltration — observed in the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign and extends coverage explicitly to the VSCode extension host surface, which the generic control does not address.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement continuous monitoring of third-party components for indicators
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Within 72 hours, the Threat Intelligence function must onboard the compromised maintainer account identifier confirmed by Wiz Research as responsible for the prior @antv wave and the 2026-05-19 new waves as a tracked threat actor indicator, and must configure automated watchlist alerts on any new npm package publish, GitHub repository creation, or GitHub Actions release attributed to that account or accounts sharing its commit signing metadata. Alerts must route to the Supply Chain Security triage queue with a P2 SLA of four hours to review and block. The Threat Intelligence Lead is accountable; evidence of completion is the watchlist rule in the threat intelligence platform with a documented test alert and confirmed routing to the triage queue.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires monitoring for unexpected behavioural change in third-party components; this adaptation operationalises the specific intelligence that the same compromised maintainer account drove multiple waves of Mini Shai-Hulud activity, enabling proactive interdiction of future waves rather than reactive detection.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and maintain a third-party incident response procedure
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Third-Party Risk function must invoke the formal supply chain incident response procedure within 6 hours of this advisory, designating the Mini Shai-Hulud / TeamPCP wave of 2026-05-19 as an active TPR-4TH severity-3 incident, and must notify all internal teams with confirmed or possible use of @antv-scoped packages, size-sensor, echarts-for-react, timeago.js, actions-cool/issues-helper, or the affected VSCode extension, as well as any downstream customers or partners whose pipelines may have ingested malicious versions during the 24-hour compromise window. Escalation must reach CISO and Legal within the same 6-hour window to assess public disclosure obligations given that secrets from internal CI/CD environments may have been dumped to public GitHub repositories. The Third-Party Risk Manager is accountable; evidence of completion is a time-stamped incident record, a distribution list of notified parties, and a signed CISO/Legal acknowledgement, all filed in the incident management platform.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control defines escalation paths and notification obligations at a framework level; this adaptation specifies the exact incident trigger (2026-05-19 TeamPCP wave), the named affected ecosystem components, the 6-hour escalation clock, and the specific legal-disclosure risk created by secrets exfiltrated to public repositories.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Require credential rotation and access revocation procedures for
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: All CI/CD pipeline secrets — including npm publish tokens, GitHub Actions runner tokens, and any cloud-provider credentials — present in any GitHub Actions workflow that referenced actions-cool/issues-helper or any of the 317 malicious @antv-ecosystem package versions prior to 2026-05-19 must be rotated and the old credentials revoked within 12 hours of this advisory; rotation must be confirmed by the owning team lead against a checklist of affected repositories identified from the dependency inventory produced under AC-TPR-005-SNC-01. Where secrets are confirmed or suspected to have been dumped to public GitHub repositories, the repository exposure must be reported to GitHub Security simultaneously with rotation, and any downstream systems authenticating with those credentials must be notified of the revocation. The Head of Platform Engineering is accountable; evidence of completion is a credential-rotation log with old-token revocation timestamps and, where applicable, GitHub Security ticket numbers, submitted to the incident record within the 12-hour window.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires credential rotation and access revocation upon detection of supply chain compromise in general terms; this adaptation enforces a 12-hour deadline, names the specific credential types (npm tokens, GitHub Actions runner tokens, cloud credentials) at risk from the Mini Shai-Hulud exfiltration path, and mandates GitHub Security reporting for the confirmed public-repository secret-dump vector.

incidents.headline: NYC Health + Hospitals breach exposes biometrics of 1.8 million via vendor
incidents.summary: New York City Health and Hospitals Corporation (NYCHHC), the largest public health system in the US, disclosed on May 19, 2026 that an unauthorized actor accessed its systems via a compromised third-party vendor between approximately November 25, 2025, and February 11, 2026, for roughly 11 weeks. Attackers copied files containing highly sensitive personal, medical, financial, and biometric data including fingerprints and palm prints belonging to at least 1.8 million patients and employees. The breach was reported to the US Department of Health and Human Services on March 24, 2026, and public notices were posted to the NYCHHC website on or around May 19, 2026.

secondary_mappings.why: Breach exposed irreplaceable biometric data (fingerprints, palm prints), PHI, geolocation, and financial information for 1.8 million individuals, triggering HIPAA and state privacy obligations.
Initial access was gained through an unnamed third-party vendor breach, highlighting third-party risk management failure.
NYCHHC reported the breach to the HHS Office for Civil Rights on March 24, 2026, triggering HIPAA breach notification obligations affecting 1.8 million individuals.

incidents.severity_rationale: 1.8 million records compromised including irreplaceable biometric identifiers (fingerprints, palm prints), full medical records, SSNs, and financial data; one of the largest healthcare breaches of 2026 with permanent harm potential for affected individuals.

sources.title: Biometrics, diagnoses, and bank details exposed in major healthcare breach

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure that identity and access management controls for all users — including third-party vendors — prevent unauthorised access to systems containing sensitive personal, medical, and biometric data.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Establish and maintain an enterprise-wide cybersecurity policy that governs access management, third-party risk, and incident detection.
Implement multi-factor authentication and least-privilege access controls for all users with access to sensitive systems and data.
Deploy continuous monitoring and anomaly-detection capabilities to identify unauthorised access or lateral movement within enterprise networks.
Establish and enforce vendor and third-party access governance, including time-limited credentials, access reviews, and contractual security requirements.
Maintain an incident response plan that includes defined procedures for breach detection, containment, eradication, and regulatory notification.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and enforce vendor and third-party access governance
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: NYC Health + Hospitals Corporation must immediately audit all active third-party vendor credentials with access to NYCHHC systems, revoke any credentials not re-validated within 72 hours of this directive, and require the implicated vendor — and all vendors with equivalent access scope — to re-execute access agreements containing explicit session-logging obligations, 30-day maximum credential rotation, and contractual breach-notification windows of no more than 72 hours. The CISO and Chief Procurement Officer are jointly accountable; evidence of completion is a timestamped vendor access register showing zero unvalidated third-party credentials and countersigned contract amendments on file.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control establishes a general vendor governance framework; this adaptation targets the specific failure mode — an unnamed third-party vendor whose compromised credentials gave attackers approximately 11 weeks of undetected access to NYCHHC systems between November 25, 2025 and February 11, 2026 — by imposing concrete re-validation timelines and hardened contractual obligations on that specific vendor class.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Deploy continuous monitoring and anomaly-detection capabilities to identify
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: NYCHHC must deploy retroactive log analysis across all systems accessible via the compromised vendor pathway for the full window of November 25, 2025 through February 11, 2026, generating a complete lateral-movement and data-exfiltration map; going forward, NYCHHC must configure real-time anomaly-detection rules that alert within four hours whenever any third-party credential initiates bulk file-copy operations exceeding a defined data-volume threshold against repositories containing biometric, medical, or financial records. The CISO is accountable, and evidence of completion is a signed forensic report covering the 11-week compromise window and documented SIEM rule configurations with test-triggered alert logs.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control calls for general continuous monitoring; this adaptation addresses the specific detection failure — bulk copying of biometric and medical files by a vendor-credentialed actor went undetected for roughly 11 weeks — by mandating both a retroactive forensic closure of that gap and a forward-looking rule tuned to the exact exfiltration behaviour observed.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement multi-factor authentication and least-privilege access controls for
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: NYCHHC must enforce multi-factor authentication on every third-party vendor account with access to any system storing biometric identifiers, EHR data, or financial records — including the vendor implicated in the November 2025–February 2026 breach — and must reduce vendor account privileges to the minimum data-scope necessary for contractual deliverables, validated by a formal access review completed within 30 days of this directive. The Chief Information Officer is accountable; evidence of completion is an MFA enrollment report showing 100% coverage for vendor accounts and a privilege-reduction log signed off by each system data owner.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control states a broad MFA and least-privilege requirement; this adaptation anchors it to the demonstrated gap — a third-party vendor credential was compromised and used without apparent MFA enforcement to access repositories containing the fingerprints and palm prints of 1.8 million NYCHHC patients and employees — specifying the exact account class and data types that must be protected.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain an incident response plan that includes defined
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: NYCHHC must conduct a formal post-incident review of the timeline gap between the confirmed breach start date of November 25, 2025 and the HHS notification date of March 24, 2026 — approximately 120 days — documenting each phase of detection, containment, and notification with accountable owners, and must update its incident response plan to establish a maximum 30-day internal detection-to-notification target for breaches involving biometric or PHI data, with a mandatory HIPAA Breach Notification Rule compliance checkpoint at day 45 post-discovery. The General Counsel and CISO are jointly accountable; evidence of completion is a board-approved revised IRP and a published after-action report filed with NYC Department of Health.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires a generic incident response plan; this adaptation forces NYCHHC to confront the specific 120-day gap between breach onset and federal notification in the context of a biometric and PHI breach affecting 1.8 million individuals, embedding concrete time-bounded notification milestones calibrated to HIPAA obligations into the plan itself.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and maintain an enterprise-wide cybersecurity policy that
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: NYCHHC must revise its enterprise cybersecurity policy within 60 days to include an explicit biometric data protection standard — covering fingerprint and palm-print data held in NYCHHC systems — that mandates encryption at rest and in transit, restricts third-party access to biometric repositories to named individuals with documented business justification, and cross-references New York SHIELD Act obligations and HIPAA safeguard requirements; the policy revision must be ratified by the NYCHHC Board of Directors and communicated to all vendors with system access. The CISO is the drafting authority; evidence of completion is the board resolution approving the revised policy and signed vendor acknowledgment receipts.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control calls for a general enterprise cybersecurity policy covering third-party risk; this adaptation addresses the specific gap revealed by this incident — the absence of a biometric-specific data governance standard that would have limited a vendor's ability to bulk-copy fingerprint and palm-print files — anchoring the policy update to the named data types, the named regulatory frameworks applicable to NYCHHC as a New York public health entity, and the named failure mode.

incidents.headline: Grafana Labs GitHub source code stolen via TanStack npm supply chain attack
incidents.summary: Grafana Labs disclosed on May 16, 2026, and updated its investigation findings on May 19, 2026, that attackers exploited the TanStack npm supply chain attack (TeamPCP's 'Mini Shai-Hulud' campaign) on May 11 to steal a GitHub workflow token and exfiltrate the company's entire codebase and internal repository contents. The attackers issued a ransom demand on May 16 threatening to release the codebase, which Grafana publicly refused to pay; no customer production systems or Grafana Cloud data were compromised. The same supply chain attack wave also hit OpenAI, Mistral AI, and over 170 npm/PyPI packages.

secondary_mappings.why: The breach originated from a compromised open-source npm dependency (TanStack) in Grafana's CI/CD pipeline, representing a fourth-party supply chain risk.
Attackers exfiltrated Grafana Labs' full codebase including private source code, threatening competitive IP exposure and potential future vulnerability discovery by adversaries.
Grafana's public refusal to pay the ransom and proactive disclosure management became a high-profile reputational moment within the open-source security community.

incidents.severity_rationale: Source code and internal operational data were exfiltrated and subject to an extortion demand, but no customer production systems or Grafana Cloud platform data were compromised; impact is primarily IP loss and reputational risk.
incidents.threat_actor: TeamPCP (CoinbaseCartel listed as extortionist on dark web site per SecurityWeek)

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure that software supply chain dependencies, CI/CD pipeline credentials, and third-party package integrations are continuously monitored, validated for integrity, and protected against unauthorised exfiltration of proprietary source code and operational data.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Establish and maintain a software supply chain security programme covering third-party package integrity verification, dependency provenance validation, and CI/CD pipeline access controls.
Implement privileged access management and short-lived credential controls for all CI/CD pipeline tokens, GitHub workflow secrets, and automated deployment credentials.

Deploy continuous monitoring and anomaly detection across source code repositories and developer tooling environments, including canary-token-based detection for unauthorised access to sensitive code assets.

incidents.headline: Rhysida ransomware claims attack on Stuttgart city government
incidents.summary: On May 19, 2026, the Rhysida ransomware group posted Landeshauptstadt Stuttgart — the capital city of Baden-Württemberg, Germany — as a victim on its dark web leak site, claiming responsibility for a cyberattack against the city's municipal IT infrastructure (stuttgart.de). The group threatened to disclose sensitive municipal data if negotiations are not initiated; Stuttgart city authorities had not issued a public statement within the disclosure window. The specific systems encrypted or data volumes exfiltrated had not been confirmed by the victim as of May 20, 2026.

secondary_mappings.why: A ransomware attack on a city government threatens continuity of public services and municipal IT operations for Stuttgart's ~630,000 residents.
As an EU public body, Stuttgart is subject to GDPR mandatory breach notification within 72 hours of confirmed personal data compromise.

incidents.severity_rationale: A state-capital city government targeted by a prolific ransomware group with a data-leak threat carries significant public-sector and civic data risk, though impact scope and encryption confirmation remain unverified as of disclosure.

sources.title: Rhysida Ransomware Targets Landeshauptstadt Stuttgart
[RHYSIDA] - Ransomware Victim: Landeshauptstadt Stuttgart
Victim: Landeshauptstadt Stuttgart - Rhysida

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure the organisation can detect, contain, and eradicate ransomware-class intrusions before threat actors achieve double-extortion leverage over sensitive data.
Ensure sensitive data processed and stored in municipal IT environments is protected against unauthorised exfiltration by external threat actors.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Implement and maintain endpoint detection and response (EDR) capabilities across all enterprise and operational technology endpoints.
Implement privileged access management (PAM) controls to restrict and monitor administrative account usage across critical systems.
Maintain tested, offline, and immutable backup and recovery capabilities sufficient to restore critical systems without paying a ransom.
Establish and rehearse a cyber incident response plan that includes ransomware-specific playbooks, regulatory notification workflows, and stakeholder communication procedures.
Implement network segmentation and zero-trust architecture controls to limit lateral movement by threat actors following initial compromise.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement and maintain endpoint detection and response (EDR)
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Landeshauptstadt Stuttgart IT Security Office must, within 24 hours of this advisory (by end of day 20 May 2026), verify that EDR agents are actively reporting telemetry on all endpoints within the stuttgart.de domain, with particular focus on any systems supporting citizen-facing municipal services and internal administration. Where EDR coverage gaps are identified, affected endpoints must be isolated from the network pending agent deployment or manual forensic triage. Evidence of completion is a signed coverage report showing ≥98% enrolled endpoints with live telemetry, submitted to the CISO and the Baden-Württemberg Landesamt für Verfassungsschutz liaison.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: This adaptation moves beyond generic EDR maintenance to mandate an immediate, time-bound coverage audit of the stuttgart.de environment specifically, triggered by Rhysida's claimed access, and defines the isolation response for any gaps discovered during active potential compromise.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement privileged access management (PAM) controls to restrict
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: By 20 May 2026 17:00 CET, the Stuttgart municipal IT team must force-rotate credentials for all privileged and administrative accounts operating within the stuttgart.de Active Directory environment and any connected systems, treating all current privileged session tokens as potentially compromised given the unconfirmed access window. All non-emergency privileged sessions must be suspended until re-authenticated under emergency PAM procedures, and any accounts exhibiting anomalous lateral movement or authentication patterns since 1 April 2026 — Rhysida's typical dwell-time window — must be disabled and escalated for forensic review. Accountability rests with the Head of IT Operations; completion is evidenced by a PAM platform log extract confirming bulk credential rotation and a list of suspended accounts delivered to the incident response team lead.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: This adaptation addresses the specific Rhysida threat actor's documented behaviour of leveraging compromised administrative credentials for lateral movement, and anchors the credential rotation to the realistic earliest-compromise date given Rhysida's known dwell time, rather than treating it as a routine periodic task.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain tested
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: By 21 May 2026, the Stuttgart municipal IT recovery team must verify the integrity and availability of offline or immutable backups for the systems most critical to city operations — including citizen registration (Einwohnermeldeamt), financial management, and public safety dispatch systems — confirming that the most recent validated snapshot predates any plausible Rhysida intrusion and has not been reachable from the compromised stuttgart.de network segment. The recovery team must execute a documented restore test for at least one critical system in an isolated environment and confirm the recovery time objective can be met without decrypting any Rhysida-held data. The CISO must receive a written recovery-readiness declaration, signed by the IT Operations Head, no later than 22 May 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: This adaptation targets the specific operational systems whose unavailability would most severely harm Stuttgart residents, mandates a pre-intrusion timestamp check to detect whether backups themselves may have been tampered with during Rhysida's dwell period, and establishes a hard deadline aligned with the active ransom negotiation window.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and rehearse a cyber incident response plan
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Landeshauptstadt Stuttgart Pressestelle, in coordination with the CISO and the Oberbürgermeister's office, must publish an initial public statement acknowledging the Rhysida dark web claim by 20 May 2026 18:00 CET, and must separately notify the Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik (BSI) and the Baden-Württemberg Landesdatenschutzbeauftragter within the 72-hour GDPR breach notification window running from the time the claim was first identified. Internal stakeholder communications — covering affected municipal departments, political leadership, and public service continuity leads — must be distributed using an out-of-band channel (not stuttgart.de email infrastructure) given potential adversary visibility. Accountability for this action sits with the city's designated Incident Commander; evidence of completion is a timestamped BSI notification receipt and a log of out-of-band stakeholder distribution.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: This adaptation operationalises the communication and notification obligations specific to a German municipal entity under GDPR and BSI reporting requirements, names the exact regulatory bodies, enforces the use of out-of-band channels given the live compromise, and sets a concrete deadline tied to the public disclosure event on 19 May 2026.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement network segmentation and zero-trust architecture controls to
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Within 6 hours of this advisory, the Stuttgart network operations team must implement emergency micro-segmentation rules to isolate the core stuttgart.de server infrastructure — particularly application servers, database tiers, and Active Directory domain controllers — from lateral network access by workstation subnets and any third-party municipal service provider connections, until forensic scope confirmation is complete. All east-west traffic within the municipal data centre must be logged at the firewall level and reviewed for indicators consistent with Rhysida's known toolset (including PsExec, WMI-based execution, and RDP traversal) covering the period from 1 April 2026 to present. The Head of Network Infrastructure is accountable; completion evidence is a change-log entry for the segmentation rule deployment and a threat-hunting summary report delivered to the incident response team within 48 hours.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: This adaptation targets the specific lateral movement techniques attributed to Rhysida operators and applies emergency segmentation to the named stuttgart.de infrastructure rather than treating segmentation as a long-cycle architecture project, directly countering the threat actor's propagation model during an active and unscoped compromise.

incidents.headline: Meta begins companywide 8,000-job layoff wave on May 20
incidents.summary: Meta Platforms commenced a company-wide redundancy round on 20 May 2026, eliminating approximately 8,000 employees — roughly 10% of its 78,865-person global workforce — across all major business units including Reality Labs, Facebook, recruiting, sales, and global operations. Chief People Officer Janelle Gale notified affected staff by email on May 20, citing the need to 'run the company more efficiently' and offset $115–135 billion in 2026 AI infrastructure investment; a further 6,000 open roles are also being cancelled. US employees receive 16 weeks of base pay plus two weeks per year of tenure and 18 months of health coverage; Reuters reports additional cuts are planned for H2 2026.

secondary_mappings.why: Layoffs are explicitly framed as a structural pivot to an AI-first operating model, representing a fundamental reshaping of Meta's business architecture.
Mass elimination of 10% of workforce while posting record revenue and profits has drawn significant employee, media, and investor scrutiny over corporate responsibility.
The restructuring is directly driven by AI-led productivity gains enabling headcount reduction across engineering, operations, and support functions.

incidents.severity_rationale: Affects approximately 8,000 employees globally across all divisions of one of the world's five largest companies by market capitalisation, with further H2 2026 cuts signalled; reputational and labour-market impact is substantial.
incidents.financial_impact_disclosed: Annualised savings estimated at $7–8 billion (Bank of America projection); severance costs not separately disclosed

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure the organisation maintains sufficient skilled workforce capacity to execute strategic objectives during and after planned headcount reductions.
Ensure workforce reduction programmes comply with applicable employment law, are executed with procedural fairness, and protect the organisation from material legal and reputational liability.

adaptive_master_controls.statement: Maintain a workforce planning framework that aligns headcount decisions with strategic capacity requirements.
Ensure all involuntary separation events comply with applicable statutory notice, severance, and non-discrimination obligations.
Establish and maintain a critical-role retention programme to safeguard key talent during organisational restructuring.
Ensure HR processes governing mass workforce events are documented, auditable, and subject to independent review.
Implement structured internal communication protocols for workforce reduction events to manage employee relations and reputational risk.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Maintain a workforce planning framework that aligns headcount
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Chief People Officer must produce, within 30 days of the 20 May 2026 layoff notification, a board-reviewed capacity model demonstrating that the elimination of approximately 8,000 roles — spanning Reality Labs, Facebook product, recruiting, sales, and global operations — does not degrade delivery capability against the $115–135 billion 2026 AI infrastructure programme. The model must map each affected business unit's headcount reduction to residual capacity ratios and flag any unit where post-RIF staffing falls below the minimum required to sustain committed AI infrastructure milestones. The Chief People Officer and Chief Financial Officer are jointly accountable; evidence of completion is a signed capacity attestation tabled at the next scheduled Board People & Culture Committee meeting.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires alignment between headcount decisions and strategic capacity requirements; this adaptation forces explicit reconciliation of the specific May 2026 RIF scale against Meta's concurrent $115–135B AI infrastructure spend, which creates an unusually acute tension between cost reduction and programme delivery that a generic workforce planning framework would not surface.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Ensure all involuntary separation events comply with applicable statutory notice
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Employment Legal and HR Compliance teams must, by 27 May 2026, complete a jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction statutory compliance matrix covering every country in which the 20 May 2026 RIF affects employees, validating that the US severance package (16 weeks base pay plus two weeks per year of tenure, 18 months health coverage) satisfies or exceeds applicable WARN Act thresholds and that equivalent local-law minima are met in all non-US jurisdictions. For any jurisdiction where a statutory works council consultation, collective agreement approval, or advance notification period has not yet been satisfied, the matrix must record the outstanding obligation, the legal deadline, and the name of the accountable regional HR director. The completed matrix must be reviewed by outside employment counsel and retained as an auditable record.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control addresses statutory notice, severance, and non-discrimination obligations generically; this adaptation anchors compliance verification to the specific US severance terms announced on 20 May 2026 and to the multi-jurisdictional exposure created by a simultaneous global RIF, where WARN Act compliance in the US does not discharge obligations under EU, UK, or APAC collective redundancy regimes.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and maintain a critical-role retention programme to
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: By 30 May 2026, the heads of Reality Labs, Facebook AI Research, and Meta's AI Infrastructure engineering function must each submit to the Chief People Officer a named critical-role register identifying roles deemed essential to the 2026 AI infrastructure programme that are at elevated flight-risk following the 20 May RIF wave, together with a retention action — such as accelerated vesting, retention bonus, or confirmed role elevation — committed for each named individual. Given that Reuters has reported further H2 2026 cuts, the register must be refreshed monthly until 31 December 2026, with any departures of critical-role holders escalated to the Chief People Officer within 48 hours. Evidence of completion is the signed registers and documented retention commitments held in Meta's HRIS.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control establishes a generic critical-role retention programme; this adaptation targets the specific AI infrastructure units most exposed by the May 2026 RIF and embeds a monthly refresh cadence driven by the confirmed H2 2026 second-wave risk, which a standing programme would not automatically address.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Ensure HR processes governing mass workforce events are documented
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Internal Audit function must initiate, no later than 10 June 2026, an independent process review of the selection methodology used to identify the approximately 8,000 employees notified on 20 May 2026, with particular scrutiny of whether role-elimination decisions across Reality Labs, recruiting, sales, and global operations were applied consistently and free from protected-characteristic bias. The review must examine the demographic composition of the affected population against the pre-RIF workforce profile and produce a written findings report — including any remediation items — delivered to the Audit & Risk Committee no later than 31 July 2026. The Head of Internal Audit is accountable; the report must be retained for a minimum of seven years as an auditable artefact.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires that HR processes for mass workforce events be auditable and subject to independent review; this adaptation specifies the trigger (the 20 May 2026 notification event), the precise scope (selection methodology and demographic bias across named business units), the reviewing body, and the delivery deadline, converting a standing governance requirement into a time-bound post-RIF assurance action.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement structured internal communication protocols for workforce reduction
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Chief Communications Officer must, within five business days of 20 May 2026, conduct a post-send audit of the Janelle Gale employee notification email to assess whether the stated rationale — 'run the company more efficiently' as an offset to AI infrastructure investment — was received by remaining employees as consistent with prior leadership messaging, and identify any communication gaps that are generating attrition risk or productivity disruption signals in internal listening channels (e.g. Workplace by Meta, engagement pulse surveys). A structured communication plan addressing identified gaps must be approved by the CEO and distributed to all remaining employees no later than 6 June 2026, and the effectiveness of that communication must be re-measured via a targeted pulse survey by 30 June 2026. The Chief Communications Officer and Chief People Officer are jointly accountable; survey results and the audit memo are the evidence artefacts.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The master control requires structured internal communication protocols for workforce reduction events; this adaptation is anchored to the specific framing used in the 20 May Gale email — linking layoffs explicitly to AI infrastructure offsetting — which introduces a distinct reputational and employee-relations risk that generic communication protocols do not address, namely the perception that headcount is being sacrificed to fund capital expenditure.

incidents.headline: RMT suspends London Tube driver strike after TfL concession
incidents.summary: The Rail, Maritime and Transport (RMT) union suspended planned 24-hour London Underground driver walkouts scheduled for 19–20 May and 21–22 May 2026, following last-minute talks with Transport for London (TfL) on 18 May in which TfL shifted its position on the contested 'compressed four-day working week' roster. The dispute — centring on RMT claims that the compressed scheduling increases driver fatigue and compromises network safety — remains unresolved, with the union warning further strike action will follow and provisionally scheduling new walkout dates of 2–3 June and 4–5 June 2026. The April 2026 strikes had already caused network-wide disruption across the London Underground for the first time since September 2025.

secondary_mappings.why: The RMT's core grievance is that compressed four-day rosters increase driver fatigue and create an unsafe working environment, giving the dispute an explicit health and safety dimension.
Threatened network shutdown of the London Underground — a critical public transport artery — created city-wide business continuity risk that was only averted by the last-minute suspension.

incidents.severity_rationale: Strike suspension averted immediate disruption to millions of daily commuters, but the underlying dispute is unresolved and further industrial action is scheduled for June 2026, sustaining ongoing operational and reputational risk for TfL.

historical_analogues.event_name: RMT South Western Railway guards dispute

historical_analogues.outcome: Night Tube launch delayed six months; TfL conceded revised rostering terms under ACAS mediation, establishing a direct precedent for the 2026 dispute.
